headline: Government backbenchers proposed 3 private member bills — all blocked
context: Three private member bills proposed by government backbenchers in 2025 were blocked from reaching debate by the government whip, undermining parliamentary independence.

cards.big: 3 private member bills from government's own MPs blocked by government whip
cards.sub: Even ruling coalition backbenchers are silenced when they show independence

cards.big: Standing Order 49 allows government business to take priority — private bills get remainder
cards.sub: Government controls calendar so completely that private bills effectively never reach debate

cards.big: Blocked bills included mandatory fiscal responsibility act worth potential RM12 billion in savings
cards.sub: Good governance legislation killed by party discipline before it could be debated

cards.big: UK Parliament passed 14 private member bills in 2025 session
cards.sub: Mature parliaments create space for backbench legislation even from government side

cards.big: When backbenchers can't even propose laws, Parliament is a voting machine, not a legislature
Should dedicated parliamentary time be reserved for private member bills regardless of party?
